previous_statement: P^2=P. Work in End(B(H)_sa) (node 1.1.2). By GT-funcalc the function calculus X|->f(X) is multiplicative: for f=g=x^(-1/2) on ||X-I||<1, f(S^2)g(S^2)=(fg)(S^2)=(x^(-1))(S^2)=(S^2)^(-1); since R=f(S^2) is well-defined (node 1.2, ||S^2-I||<1), this gives R^2=(S^2)^(-1). Also R=f(S^2) is a norm-limit of polynomials in S^2 (GT-funcalc) so it commutes with S, multiplication being jointly continuous (GT-funcalc, Kitaev:642). Set sgn(S):=S R; then sgn(S)^2=S R S R=S^2 R^2=I. With P:=(I+sgn(S))/2 (=theta(2Phi-I), GT-kitaev-spectral), P^2=(I+2 sgn(S)+sgn(S)^2)/4=(I+2 sgn(S)+I)/4=(I+sgn(S))/2=P. Uses nodes 1.1.2, 1.2.
new_statement: P^2=P. Work in the Banach algebra End(B(H)_sa) (node 1.1.2). For S=2Phi-I, ||S^2-I||<=4 eta<1 (node 1.1). Kitaev states sgn(X)^2=I for every X with ||X^2-I||<1 (GT-kitaev-spectral, refs/kitaev:514-522, line 518), where sgn(X)=X(X^2)^(-1/2); applied to X=S, sgn(S)^2=I. Moreover Kitaev's Banach-algebra Proposition prop_P (refs/kitaev:524-532), instantiated at Phi in End(B(H)_sa) with ||Phi^2-Phi||<=eta<1/4 (def almost-idempotent, eta_0<1/4), states directly that P=theta(2Phi-I)=(1/2)(I+sgn(2Phi-I)) satisfies P^2=P. Explicitly, P=(I+sgn(S))/2 gives P^2=(I+2 sgn(S)+sgn(S)^2)/4=(I+sgn(S))/2=P. Uses nodes 1.1, 1.1.2.
